Home

Flash Alert: EtherRat and TukTuk C2 End in The Gentleman Ransomware

Key Takeaways

The EtherRAT malware family was first reported by Sysdig back in December 2025. Recent intrusions show a new variant being used as initial access for The Gentleman ransomware affiliate.

The intrusion began with T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application targeting a known CVE, followed by execution of T1059.001 PowerShell payloads.

Indicators

EtherRAT C2:
185.220.100.255

TukTuk C2:
194.165.16.77

MITRE ATT&CK

Exploit Public-Facing Application - T1190
PowerShell - T1059.001
Ingress Tool Transfer - T1105
Data Encrypted for Impact - T1486
